    "answer": "Microsoft's FY2025 financial health was strong on profitability and liquidity, but increasingly capital-intensive. Profitability was excellent: FY2025 revenue was $281.724 billion, gross margin was $193.893 billion, operating income was $128.528 billion, net income was $101.832 billion, and diluted EPS was $13.64 (FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 8, Income Statements). Liquidity and capital structure remained conservative: cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments were $94.565 billion at June 30, 2025, while total debt carrying value was $43.151 billion and there was no short-term debt outstanding (FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 8, Balance Sheets; Note 10, Debt). Capital allocation remained shareholder-friendly but balanced against growth investment: Microsoft repurchased 31 million shares for $13.0 billion in FY2025 and declared dividends totaling $24.7 billion (FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Liquidity and Capital Resources, Share Repurchases and Dividends; Part II, Item 8, Note 15, Stockholders' Equity). Backlog was substantial: revenue allocated to remaining performance obligations was $375 billion at June 30, 2025, including $368 billion commercial, with approximately 40% expected to be recognized over the next 12 months (FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 8, Note 12, Unearned Revenue). Material headwinds are explicit: contractual obligations totaled $397.045 billion, including $178.701 billion of operating and finance lease obligations and $109.953 billion of purchase commitments, primarily related to datacenters (FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Material Cash Requirements and Other Obligations). LinkedIn: AI likely improves engagement and monetization if it increases matching and marketing productivity; LinkedIn revenue grew 9% in FY2025 and 11% in constant currency in Q2 FY2026, while Satya Nadella cited double-digit member growth and 30% growth in paid video ads (FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Segment Results of Operations, 'Productivity and Business Processes'; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Satya Nadella prepared remarks; Amy Hood prepared remarks). Search and news advertising: AI can create demand through Copilot and intent capture, but substitution and execution risks are visible; FY2025 search and news advertising revenue ex-TAC grew 20%, yet Q2 FY2026 ex-TAC growth moderated to 10% in constant currency and was slightly below expectations because of execution challenges (FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Segment Results of Operations, 'More Personal Computing'; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks). Windows and Devices: AI PCs are a potential demand catalyst because the 10-K lists growth of the AI PC category as a factor affecting Windows OEM revenue, and Windows 11 users surpassed 1 billion, up over 45% year over year, but Q2 FY2026 guidance expected Windows OEM revenue to decline roughly 10% as Windows 10 end-of-support benefits and elevated inventory normalize (FY2025 10-K, Part I, Item 1, More Personal Computing, Windows and Devices; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Satya Nadella prepared remarks; Amy Hood prepared remarks).
